Name: MiniPACS
//: With 7.6GB RAM available on the host we can give SQLite a 256MB in-process page cache, on top of OS page cache.
//-mmap-: mmap the SQLite index so OS page cache serves it zero-copy. Ignored on older Orthanc builds, safe.
//-http-threads-: Bumped from 10 -> 50. OHIF open fires ~150 parallel HTTP requests (metadata + frames). 10 threads serialized them; 50 fits comfortably in 7.6GB RAM (~2MB stack each).

DicomWeb.//-studies-: MainDicomTags = Orthanc serves the tags cached in SQLite — no disk reads.
DicomWeb.//-series-: Was 'Full' — that made Orthanc open every DICOM file on disk to harvest every tag on each metadata request. At 295-instance MRI studies that was 22s per waiting HTTP and ~5 min to fully open a study in OHIF. 'MainDicomTags' returns the cached set (what OHIF actually uses to lay out the viewer) from the SQLite index instantly; per-instance full metadata is still available via individual WADO calls when truly needed.
DicomWeb.//-cache-: Explicitly cache the computed metadata list per series so we only rebuild it when the series changes.
